body:
Daughter's in Dorchester, MA, wondering why the hell so many people are honking and screaming up and down her street. Turns out that she lives in the largest area of Cabo Verdean expats in the US.

"But when will they STOP?!!" she asks. :D
As an Australian with a long memory and a grudge bearing disposition, it is absolutely wonderful watching Uruguay having a completely cursed time this world cup.
Good luck Spain, Uruguay is going to kick the shit out of them all game. They're in full meltdown mode.
One of my coworkers is from Cabo Verde. He was on video call with his family that still live there during the game against Spain. Can't wait to talk to him tomorrow about this one

Uruguay are in danger of leaving the World Cup on 2 points. There is a non-zero chance if Spain tie Uruguay that Cape Verde top the group should they too be thrashing Saudi Arabia, meaning Spain might want to ensure they get the win.

It would be hilarious if Cape Verde finishes top of this group just like how Japan did in 2022.

They're the underdog story of this WC.

Getting 2 draws against former World Champions is no mere coincidence, they have deserved it.
What a fucking result for Cabo Verde, this has to be their peak in terms of results go right?
So “dark horse” teams that can make deep runs but still not win the thing is really just a fancy way of saying overated af. Look at Belgium, Türkiye, now Uruguay. lol
With a little bit luck Cape Verde might actually win their group wtf
Last day of this group gonna be fun lol
Think Cape Verde manages a draw against Saudi while Spain beats Uruguay… pushing Cape Verde through in 2nd with 3 points

Just got home from the game. As a neutral fan I couldn’t have asked for more. Great game, multiple goals, exciting moments… it’s the first soccer game I’ve ever been to in person and it was great. The fans for both sides were great, especially Cabo Verde (it was clear Uruguay fans were battling disappointment while the CV fans were excited for everything). 

That written… there were huge swaths of section where food and drink normally are for football games (been to the stadium many times for the Hurricanes and Dolphins) that were completely blocked off to VIP only. It was kind of crazy. So… A+ for the game and pageantry and atmosphere, and F for the “VIP” requirements.
I was at the match. Was that as good on tv is it was in person? Unbelievable scenes

That second CV goal was defensive error but the finish still took some doing. Think we’ve got a great chance to not only grab a debut W but a spot in the next round. Think I gotta gamble and schedule the time off!

Referee should have let that free kick at the last minute happen. 

I don't understand how he ended it there.
Cape Verde (against *Spain* and Uruguay) and Curacao get points and perennial Dark Horse Turkiye get nothing against Australia and Paraguay. Embarrassing.

Congratulations to Cape Verde on this historic performance.

Being unbeaten against Spain and Uruguay is no mean feat and they have performed exceptionally.

Probably the biggest overachievers of this WC.

I hope they reach the knockouts.
CONmebol was right there
What if Uraguay beats Spain and Cabo Verde beats Daudi Arabia. Spain would go through as a third place team. That would be wild
Costa Rica 2014
CAPE VERDE YOU JUST MADE ME YOUR FAN
Uruguay screwed up again. Damn how did Bielsa waste the potential of Uruguay is insane. Another 2002 disasterclass.
Cape Verde showing the world what they’ve been capable of. Love to see it
Please God let cape verde top the group because that would be simply too funny
Great game - Spain will be feeling better knowing its not just them. The Saudis will be shitting themselves watching that.

if Bielsa is gonna play Valverde at the 10, he's gotta do more than pass the ball around in the back and shoot it into the stands 3 times a game. He's got insane talent and just doesnt feel like showing up for some reason. 

De la Cruz should've been in instead of either Ugarte or Bentacur cuz Valverde is just too conservative in that position.
In WC, I mean they're debuting...
Holy shit they actually did it

Spain 2-0 Uruguay

Cabo Verde 0-1 Saudi Arabia

With those results Uruguay finish 4th. Very possible
Cabo Verde is unbeatable!
I mean, sure a draw could be enough, but I think they can actually beat Saudia Arabia if they play the way they did today
The game has passed Bielsa
They are actually unstoppable.
So happy for Cabo Verde.
Blaming Bielsa for what his players did today is not very smart

Turkey is shite, but Uruguay and Belgium do have quality. Uruguay just has a bunch of hot headed dumbasses on their squad too. I think Belgium is going to come to play finally against New Zealand (unfortunately because I'd like to see the Kiwis advance... hopefully they get the win tonight!)
FRAUDmebol

Good comparison. They were also up against 3 World Champions and topped their group. 

If only Tim Krul didn't enter the pitch in the Quarter-Final against them.
Watching Cape Verde game is  like witnessing a miracle unraveling.
Best game of the WC so far, great performance from Cabo Verde.
What if Uruguay beats Spain. If my grandma had wheels, she would be a bike.
Cape Verde passing game reminds me of joga bonito
Uruguay always does this shit, fuck them
Crazy game lol. CONMEBOL is looking terrible fr tho
When the United States beat the Uruguay 5-1 a lot of people in the comments were saying oh well it’s just a friendly. That should have been Uruguay wake up call but it wasn’t.
Cape verde give me what Brazil used to a long time ago now, they give me that fix im looking for of crazy audacious plays, amazing bits of skill and mavericks.
They have the city, state (the official abbreviation for it),and the country all in the comment. How much more specificity could you possibly want?

Best match of the World Cup so far.

What a fun and amazing dynamic.
He ran 15 yards out of his box for no reason
Crazy how open this game was at the end. Either team could have won this
unfortunately i have zero faith in this uruguay team to beat spain
I'd just like to say I called cape Verde* as dark horses weeks ago after watching a lot of their qualifying. I am just patting myself on the back.

If you fell Bielsa is stupid, imagine Parrera who wasted a squad with Prime Ronaldinho, Ronaldo Phenomenon, Roberto Carlos, Cafu, Dida, Adriano 'The Emperor', Kaká, Robinho, Juninho.P and Gilberto Silva on 2006.

Well, at least he did won the damn thing in 94'
They probably shouldn't play Yamal lol.
And then the whole damn thing.
I have to say, that stadium they just played in is an absolute eyesore from the outside. Genuinely what the fuck is that
We were there! What a historic game and what an atmosphere! Incredible! I’ll remember this forever!!!
Never
Need Austria to win tomorrow. So they take first place in their group. Argentina takes second. And Spain vs Argentina right away in the Knockout. Only one will win the cup anyway.
Do not pat yourself on the back for your accurate spelling, however
KSA was so dogshit I could see CPV taking the points off them entirely.

> Belgium do have quality

Has it been in the room with us this tournament? Because all I see is that a past-his-best Kevin de Bruyne is still by far their most important outfield player.

> I think Belgium is going to come to play finally against New Zealand

I'm gonna laugh when they get hit by another Chris Wood masterclass
They probably need to play full strength, they were saying on the post match that if Uruguay wins and Saudi wins, Spain would end up 3rd. 2nd place would play against us if we win tomorrow.
If Cape Verde wins, they take second in the group no matter the result of Spain/Uruguay.
I yelling at the screen for a country I'd never heard of before this tourney.
One of the best World Cup games I’ve  seen
Tons of Portuguese, Azoreans etc in coastal Mass. Let the boys honk!
Spain will probably play for a tie. Cape Verde needs a big win for Spain to not be first place. If that’s the case Uruguay still have a good chance to qualify with 3 points and 0 GD
"si no ganamos jugando a la pelota, ganaremos a las patadas"
I mean even if they finish in 3rd, they ain't qualifying with 2 points
Cape Verde have exceeded all the expectations from everyone at this point. So happy to see a small nation hold their own against footballing giants. I really want them to advance to knockouts.
They’re just a mediocre team now. They are probably never winning another World Cup. 2010 was their last chance
truly heart stopping stuff
They were going to kick the shit out of Spain all game anyway. It’s what they do
THIS time? Don't you remember 2022? There's no more blessed time for us

Dude, I don't actually give a fuck if they're good or bad. The number of times we had to go to Buenos Aires to play them to qualify for the world cup across 1974 - 2006 is absolutely stupid, before considering all the attendant bullshit that they and their FA put us through. 

That they had the temerity to ask for space on our charter flight back to Sydney for the return leg of qualifying in 2005 as if they were entitled to it is staggering.
Don’t forget Ecuador
They are coming out of the African qualifiers. They are quality.
the finish required so much composure, and technique too. he hit the ball perfectly while at an angle and not saving much time. i’m so happy for you guys. well done!
Hopefully Spain humiliates Uruguay all the way home

Yeah, but to your point he won in 1994, and the 2006 lost in the quarters against a Zidane led team 

Bielsa had Argentina getting eliminated by Sweden in 2002. And now Uruguay by Cape Verde or Saudi. It’s just not the same
Ecuador makes me so mad. They are tough as fuck in the qualifiers only to implode in group stages every time
"la garra charrua"
Cale Verde is just what I call them since we're so close
And it was a very bad decision

Well, they were in the group of Cameroon in the qualifiers. Cape Verde won a decisive game against them in Praia.

The beautiful game

I am kicking myself for not getting a cheap KSA vs CV ticket here in Houston when they were cheap.
I’m just asking in general, like is drawing against Spain and Uruguay easily their best results in their history or have they had wins against top CAF teams that were better?
his finish was stone fucking cold, i would have put it over the stands 100%
It’s not a good stadium. It’s also not even in Miami.
And that's not a great time. We have to see what does Bielsa have... but 2002 trauma remains there.

3 pts and GD 0 might not be enough. Right now, I see only 3 groups with potential 3rd places with less than 4 pts. We might have more than that or less depending on Mexico and Germany wanting to still get a results even if it doesn’t matter. Group C is the only group right now where it’s possible to have 3rd placed team with 3 pts in Scotland - simply because Brazil needs the win badly, so they’ll try
that’s not his position

True haha! But Tunisia didn’t bring the team who qualified them…

It may be boring romps, but there's a chance at fun.

Spain appears miles better than Uruguay, but Uruguay has to win that game. A draw probably isn't going to be enough. But it's within the realm of possibility that Uruguay can win right?

If they win by 1, and Cabo Verde pulls a 2 goal win, we get Cabo Verde in 1st and Spain in THIRD (almost certainly still qualifying though)
Uruguay has nothing left now.
That's what we told her!

I was checking tickets for the CV game here in Miami every day and never saw one go below $400. It's insane. Maybe the prices are better elsewhere but I couldn't budget it.

Oh, make no mistake, it's every time. This one is especially tasty though.

i was absolutely amazed to see that, i kept thinking i was missing something.  nope. he had zero reason to gift them that goal.

I agree, I think he blew it bc of time wasting leading up so he went 'you know what'
They've done that a bunch this world cup. I like them cutting down on time wasting but cutting the action short at the end of every match is taking it too far

The official FIFA site selected me but they were $350 originally and I said no. Then I saw them again for $333 but when I went to purchase them they weren't actually available. It was not a good experience. Maybe i did something wrong.

He decided to only put two players, he should've put at least three, he trusted too much in himself when he's literally a shitty ex-keeper who shouldn't be anywhere near the national team. Against a team with Zidane, Henry at the end of his prime and Vieira. With Ribery at the beginnikg of his prime. No shame in losing to them.

I don't think Spain will put their fate in others hands, Yamal might play most of the match even and with the level Uruguay showed and their defense being bad even if Spain played Ferran again and their sub midfield they will still create lots of chances.
If Spain is smart, they play for a tie. No way Cape Verde destroys Saudi Arabia to get the sufficient GD to take first place. So, by playing for a tie, they can kick out Uruguay right away.

Their manager would do himself a world of good benching de Bruyne and Rom imho, they do look far off pace even if de Bruyne has moments of looking like his old self. The younger players look hungrier and talented enough to deserve having a go this tournament, but they're way too reliant on de Bruyne when he's on the pitch. They should still beat New Zealand regardless. Wood and that Just fella are a pretty intriguing combo, but that team as a whole is just not very good
Literally they only reason I am worried about Yamal playing, don't want him to get injured. They will play dirty especially if Spain scored and they got desperate.

I mean Spain can change the whole midfield and defense and play Ferran and still be a better team on paper than Uruguay. With that defense, I don't think Uruguay can prevent Spain from scoring especially that they will have to attack and leave lots of space in behind. I think Yamal will play and he was even supposed to play more minutes in the 3rd game to prepare himself for the knockout stages but Uruguay is known for playing dirty and he might get injured so I think playing him for 45 minutes like yesterday is enough.
Uruguay Mexico would be a good game for neutrals, It would be chaos.
Depending on how things go, a draw could be beneficial for both teams.
Spain is a million times better than Uruguay. Uruguay defense is super bad too and Yamal can play the full match if Spain needed him this time.
Alternatively if Uruguay wins 1-0, Cape Verde can top the group with a 2-0 victory
Yeah, I think the poster above meant "at least second". There's a possibility they win the group. Which would be just absolutely insane.

Both were to blame but I blame the goalkeeper more because he had another defender behind him. If Muslera retreated to goal then the defender could just go intercept Cape Verde's attacker. But because Muslera decided to go out the defender had to go on goal himself and since he cant catch the ball with his hands... well
They all looked like the three stooges

You must be delusional. Second goal is entirely because of Muslera (who should've stayed on goal), and in the first goal, he put only two players in the barrier. Read r/uruguay , no one would agree with you. This is 100% this washed up ex-keeper
Ok question, why is Muslera even the starting keeper in the first place? In 2022 he was a sub for Rochet and now Rochet got benched, idk why.
Funniest shit he won't ever come back to Rosario, he's smart to know not to fuck up his legacy in Newell's
Why did they change it up? I know they had an easy qualifying group but still
Weirdly 2nd place is the worst position in this group, because it has a date with Argentina in round of 32, so 3rd would be better for Spain

This is an interesting scenario. If Austria wins tomorrow, and Algeria-Jordan end in a draw, you'd have a scenario where it would look likely for Spain to play Argentina by winning the group.

If you were Spain knowing that, would you try against Uruguay? Knowing if you lost, you either get Austria if you're second or Mexico/Belgium/Egypt as third.
that's correct

Unfortunately all of you are wrong. First goal the two defenders literally both jump to the side allowing the ball to squeak past them. Second goal, Muslera made a stupid decision to try and recover from the absolutely mind numbing choice that defender made to pass the ball.
No way they lose to Spain unless the game is full of red cards and Spanish injuries.
